name: top
description: Fetch top/trending stories from Hacker News by score. Use this when users ask what's trending, popular, or top on HN. For newest submissions, use 'new'. For highest-rated by score, use 'best'. Returns story metadata including title, URL, score, author, and time.

limit.description: Maximum number of stories to return, between 1 and 500

name: new
description: Fetch newest story submissions to Hacker News. Use this when users want the latest posts. For top/trending stories, use 'top'. For highest-rated, use 'best'. Returns story metadata including title, URL, score, author, and time.

limit.description: Maximum number of stories to return, between 1 and 500

name: best
description: Fetch highest-rated stories from Hacker News by score. Use this when users want the best-performing stories. For top/trending (which includes more stories ranked by points), use 'top'. For newest, use 'new'. Returns story metadata including title, URL, score, author, and time.

limit.description: Maximum number of stories to return, between 1 and 500

name: ask
description: Fetch Ask HN posts from Hacker News. Use this when users ask about community questions, polls, or requests to the HN audience. For Show HN posts (projects/products), use 'show'. Returns story metadata including title, URL, score, author, and time.

name: show
description: Fetch Show HN posts from Hacker News. Use this when users want to see showcased projects, products, demos, or launches. For Ask HN posts (questions/polls), use 'ask'. Returns story metadata including title, URL, score, author, and time.

name: jobs
description: Fetch job postings from Hacker News. Use this when users are looking for job opportunities or want to see hiring threads. For the monthly 'Who is hiring?' thread listings, use 'whoishiring'. Returns job posting metadata including title, company, URL, and time.

name: item
description: Fetch full details of a single Hacker News item (story, comment, job, poll, or pollopt). Use this to get the complete content of a specific HN item. Returns all available fields for the item type. For fetching only comments on a story, use 'comments' instead.

id.description: Numeric HN item ID (e.g., 12345678). Find this from story listings or search results.

name: comments
description: Fetch comments on a Hacker News story. Use this when users want to see the discussion thread for a specific story. Returns comment metadata including author, text, time, and child comments. For fetching full item details (not just comments), use 'item' instead.

id.description: Numeric HN story ID (e.g., 12345678). Find this from story listings or search results.

limit.description: Maximum number of top-level comments to return, between 1 and 100

depth.description: Comment depth level to retrieve: 1 returns only top-level comments, 2 includes replies to top-level, etc. Higher values increase response time.

name: user
description: Fetch Hacker News user profile. Use this to look up account information including account age, karma score, and recent submissions. Requires an existing HN username (not creatable via this tool).

name: search
description: Search Hacker News stories and comments via Algolia search API. Use this when users want to find specific topics, keywords, or content on HN. Returns matching items with title, URL, score, author, and time. Supports filtering by item type, sort order, and time period.

query.description: Search keywords or phrase. Supports basic text matching.

type.description: Filter by item type: 'story' for links/text posts, 'comment' for replies, 'all' for both

sort.description: Sort results by: 'date' (newest first), 'relevance' (keyword match), 'popularity' (by points)

period.description: Time window for search: 'hour', 'day', 'week', 'month', 'year', or 'all' (no filter)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return, between 1 and 100

name: whoishiring
description: Fetch 'Who is hiring?' job postings from Hacker News monthly threads. Use this when users ask about hiring opportunities or want the monthly hiring thread listings. For general job postings (freelance, contracts, standard posts), use 'jobs' instead.

limit.description: Maximum number of job postings to return, between 1 and 100
